reason: The UI has moved from generic swiping to contextual profile decisions. The persisted collection is now profileDecisions after the dual-read, dual-write, backfill, and local retire-old phases.

phases.description: Keep the existing swipes storage path, expose generated logical path constants, and fail data-contract checks if production code adds new raw path literals.
Repositories and triggers read both swipes and profileDecisions, with swipes remaining the primary write path.
Client and backend writes mirrored new decisions into both storage paths with idempotent writes.

Dry-run-first tooling copies historical swipes into profileDecisions and compares source/target counts before apply. Dev, staging, and prod had zero current and future decisions on 2026-05-15. On 2026-05-26, a legacy-source collection-group validation found 11 dev and 138 prod legacy swipes; 11 dev and 133 prod profileDecision docs were backfilled before legacy cleanup.
profileDecisions is the primary read/write path.
Local client, Functions, rules, demo tooling, and contract metadata no longer create new swipes documents.
Local swipes fallback reads and rules are removed. Old live swipes data was deleted from dev, staging, and prod on 2026-05-26 after parity validation against profileDecisions.

guards: tool/contracts/check_schema_path_literals.mjs blocks new production raw swipes collection and trigger literals.
./tool/check_data_contract.sh runs the path literal scanner before seed and rules checks.
Live dev, staging, and prod cleanup is complete; the one-time parity, backfill, and cleanup tools were retired on 2026-06-02.
Future reintroduction is guarded by schema path literal checks, Firestore rules, and the generated decision schema.
